spec.description: Personal health and fitness metrics from Garmin wearable — steps, heart rate, sleep, HRV, stress, SpO2, and activity.
Time spent in each stress zone throughout the day. Extended time in medium (51–75) or high (76–100) stress zones impairs HRV, sleep onset, and next-day recovery capacity.
spec.title: Stress Duration Breakdown

spec.description: Aerobic and anaerobic training load accumulated over the past month. Chronic load (4-week average) builds fitness. Acute load spikes significantly above chronic baseline increase injury risk (ACWR concept).
spec.title: Monthly Training Load

spec.description: Current Garmin training status and Acute:Chronic Workload Ratio. Status: Peaking, Productive, Maintaining, Recovery, or Overreaching. Optimal ACWR: 0.8–1.3 balances adaptation with injury risk management.
spec.title: Training Status & ACWR

spec.description: Total sleep duration for the last night. Adults need 7–9 hours. Consistently under 6 hours is associated with impaired cognition, immune function, hormonal regulation, and physical recovery.
spec.title: Total Sleep Time

spec.description: Garmin exporter version and build metadata. Check this to verify you’re running the latest version. Include this info when reporting issues or requesting support.
spec.title: Exporter Version

spec.description: Number of successful Garmin Connect API authentications. A drop in this metric may indicate authentication failures, API rate limiting, credential expiry, or connectivity issues with the exporter.
spec.title: Login Status

spec.description: Hydration progress toward your daily goal. General guideline: ~35 mL per kg of body weight per day, increasing with activity level and ambient heat. Even 1–2% dehydration measurably impairs physical and cognitive performance.
spec.title: Hydration Goal Progress

spec.description: Daily hydration intake compared to your Garmin-configured goal. Track whether intake scales appropriately on training days versus rest days. Sweat rate and climate significantly affect requirements.
spec.title: Hydration Intake vs Goal

spec.description: Continuous heart rate throughout the day. Normal resting range: 60–100 bpm. Athletes may see 40–60 bpm. Peaks indicate exercise or stress; dips during sleep reflect recovery quality.
spec.title: Heart Rate Trends

spec.description: Millisecond variation between consecutive heartbeats. Higher HRV generally indicates better recovery and fitness. Typical range: 20–70 ms, though this varies widely by age and fitness level. Track trends rather than absolute values.
spec.title: HRV (Heart Rate Variability)

spec.description: Breakdown of sleep into Light, Deep, REM, and Awake periods. Adults need 7–9 hours total. Aim for 15–20% deep sleep and 20–25% REM for optimal recovery and cognitive function.
spec.title: Sleep Stages

spec.description: Garmin-calculated stress score based on HRV fluctuations. Scale: 0–25 (resting), 26–50 (low), 51–75 (medium), 76–100 (high). Chronic high stress impacts HRV, sleep quality, and recovery.
spec.title: Stress Levels

spec.description: Daily breakdown of active, sedentary, and lightly active minutes. WHO guidelines recommend 150–300 min of moderate activity per week. Prolonged sedentary periods (>30 min) increase cardiovascular risk.
spec.title: Activity Time (Active vs Sedentary)

spec.description: Total, active, and resting (BMR) calorie expenditure for the day. Resting calories vary by age, weight, and height. Active calories reflect physical activity intensity and duration.
spec.title: Calories

spec.description: Total time spent per activity type (running, cycling, swimming, etc.) within the selected time range. Useful for identifying training balance and sport-specific volume distribution.
spec.title: Activity Duration by Type

spec.description: Cumulative vigorous and moderate intensity exercise minutes per week. WHO recommends ≥150 min moderate or ≥75 min vigorous activity weekly. Vigorous minutes count double toward the target.
spec.title: Weekly Intensity Minutes

spec.description: Total steps counted for the selected period. 10,000 steps/day is a common target, though research suggests meaningful benefits begin at ~7,500 steps/day. Includes all walking, running, and movement.
spec.title: Total Steps

spec.description: Average heart rate during rest, typically measured during sleep. Normal adult range: 60–100 bpm. Lower values (40–60 bpm) suggest good cardiovascular fitness. A rising trend may signal illness or overtraining.
spec.title: Resting Heart Rate

spec.description: Garmin composite score (0–100) estimating how prepared your body is for training. Factors include sleep score, recovery time, HRV status, and training load. A score ≥70 suggests readiness for high-intensity effort.
spec.title: Training Readiness

spec.description: Estimated blood oxygen saturation via optical sensor. Normal range: 95–100%. Values of 90–94% may warrant attention; below 90% is clinically concerning. Useful for altitude acclimatization or screening sleep-disordered breathing.
spec.title: Blood Oxygen (SpO₂)

spec.description: Average stress score for the day on a 0–100 scale, derived from HRV-based autonomic nervous system analysis. Scores consistently above 75 indicate elevated sympathetic activity and reduced recovery capacity.
spec.title: Avg Stress Level

spec.description: Percentage progress toward your daily step goal. Garmin auto-adjusts the goal based on recent activity history. Consistent goal achievement supports long-term cardiovascular health.
spec.title: Step Goal Progress

spec.description: How your Body Battery energy reserve changes throughout the day. Starts full after good sleep and depletes with activity and stress. Consistently low end-of-day levels suggest insufficient recovery.
spec.title: Body Battery Level Trend

spec.description: Energy gained (sleep and rest) versus energy consumed (activity and stress) over time. Net negative drain across multiple days indicates accumulated fatigue and a need for recovery or easy days.
spec.title: Body Battery Charge & Drain

spec.description: Estimated maximum oxygen consumption in mL/kg/min — a key aerobic fitness indicator. Average adults: 35–45 (women), 40–50 (men). Elite endurance athletes: 55–85. Improves with consistent aerobic training.
spec.title: VO₂ Max

spec.description: Estimated hours until your body is ready for another high-intensity effort, based on recent training load and HRV. Respecting recovery windows reduces injury risk and supports long-term performance gains.
spec.title: Recovery Time Needed

spec.description: Garmin sleep score (0–100) reflecting sleep duration, stages, and quality. Scores ≥75 indicate restorative sleep. Poor sleep scores are a leading indicator of next-day performance, mood, and stress levels.
spec.title: Readiness Sleep Score

spec.description: Estimated finish times for 5K, 10K, half marathon, and marathon distances based on current VO₂ Max and training history. Predictions improve in accuracy with more running data over time.
spec.title: Race Predictions

spec.description: Average breathing rate during waking hours in breaths per minute. Normal adult range: 12–20 breaths/min. Elevated rates can indicate respiratory illness, high stress, or altitude effects.
spec.title: Waking Respiration Rate

spec.description: Average breaths per minute during sleep. Normal range: 12–20 breaths/min. Significant fluctuations or values consistently outside this range may indicate sleep-disordered breathing.
spec.title: Sleep Respiration Rate

spec.description: Duration of detected naps (short sleep periods outside the main sleep window). Short naps (10–30 min) can improve alertness and performance. Frequent long naps may indicate insufficient nighttime sleep.
spec.title: Nap Time

spec.description: Number of movement or wakefulness events detected during sleep. Frequent restlessness disrupts slow-wave and REM sleep cycles, reducing recovery quality and next-day cognitive performance.
spec.title: Restless Moments

spec.description: Blood oxygen saturation measured via optical sensor during sleep. Normal: 95–100%. Repeated dips below 90% during sleep may indicate sleep apnea and warrant medical evaluation.
spec.title: Sleep SpO₂

spec.description: Your personal HRV benchmark values (5-day, weekly, monthly averages). Current HRV significantly below your baseline indicates incomplete recovery or an incoming illness. Trends matter more than absolute values.
spec.title: HRV Baselines

spec.description: Current Body Battery energy reserve on a 0–100 scale. >75: high readiness; 50–75: good; 25–50: moderate fatigue; <25: rest is needed before any intense activity. Recharges primarily during sleep.
spec.title: Body Battery

spec.description: Section divider or informational text panel. Use to provide context, instructions, or links relevant to adjacent panels.
Number of Garmin devices currently connected and reporting data. Multiple entries may indicate a watch paired with a bike computer, heart rate monitor, or other Garmin sensors.
spec.title: Paired Devices

spec.description: Model name of your paired Garmin device. Confirms which device is reporting data and helps identify sensor capabilities (e.g., SpO₂, ECG, body temperature) available on that model.
spec.title: Device Type

spec.description: Floors ascended and descended versus daily goal. Garmin counts one floor as ~3 meters of vertical ascent. Stair climbing is an effective cardiovascular and lower-body strength exercise.
spec.title: Floors Climbed

spec.description: Total distance covered per activity type (running, cycling, walking, etc.). Useful for tracking training volume and identifying sport-specific load distribution across a week or month.
spec.title: Activity Distance by Type

spec.description: Number of activities logged per type, plus lifetime total. Track consistency across sport disciplines and identify gaps or overemphasis in training variety over the selected period.
spec.title: Activity Count by Type

spec.description: Calories burned per activity type. Helps identify which activities contribute most to total energy expenditure and informs caloric intake needs on training versus rest days.
spec.title: Activity Calories by Type
Garmin Health & Fitness
